message:
fix(vsdk): reject malformed expected_output_hash and bind workspace replay capsules to declared input_refs

Two related hardening fixes for the workspace structural-only replay
capsule SDK:

1. validate_manifest() now requires expected_output_hash to be a
   64-character hex SHA-256 digest, rejecting malformed values before
   they reach the replay verdict comparison. The existing divergence
   test is updated to use a well-formed but wrong hash ("f"x64) so
   it still exercises the Fail verdict path.

2. replay() now calls validate_declared_input_refs() as step 3 (before
   payload/hash evaluation) to enforce that the manifest's declared
   input_refs exactly match the replayed inputs map — no missing, no
   extra, no duplicates. This prevents capsule forgery via undeclared
   input injection or declared-but-omitted inputs.

Adds is_sha256_hex helper, validate_declared_input_refs function,
BTreeSet import, and 6 targeted regression tests covering each
rejection path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
